events.title: 🌈 Greensboro Pride
events.description:
Today we honor the life and legacy of Jesse Jackson — a tireless advocate for justice, equity, and human dignity.

Rev. Jackson’s courage helped expand the moral imagination of this country. He stood on the front lines of the Civil Rights Movement, carried forward Dr. King’s vision, and never stopped pushing America to live up to its promises. Through the Rainbow PUSH Coalition and decades of organizing, he amplified the voices of people too often pushed to the margins — Black communities, working families, LGBTQ+ people, and all those fighting for equal protection under the law.

His bravery was not abstract. It was lived. He marched. He ran for president to widen political possibility. He showed up where it was uncomfortable. He spoke when it was unpopular. He insisted that dignity belongs to everyone.

At Greensboro Pride, we know our movement stands on the shoulders of leaders like Rev. Jackson who understood that liberation is interconnected. Our fight for LGBTQ+ equality is inseparable from the broader struggle for racial, economic, and social justice.

May we carry forward his legacy with the same boldness, compassion, and unshakable belief that justice is possible.

Rest in power.

🌿 Community Walk, Mental Care in Motion 👟💚

If your thoughts have felt noisy, heavy, or scattered after this stretch of bad weather, this walk is for you. Moving together, even gently, can help clear mental space and remind us we’re not alone.

✨ Ask yourself: What’s taking up my mental space right now?
Then come walk it out with us.

events.title: 🌈 Community Walk | Emotional Self-Care
events.location: Country Park – GSO, Picnic tables in Jaycee Park
events.description:
🌿 Community Walk | Emotional Self-Care 🌿
Tomorrow | Sunday, 1/18 | 10 AM

Cold weather can make emotions feel heavier, quieter, louder, or harder to name. This week’s Community Walk is an invitation to slow down, check in with yourself, and care for your emotional well-being in community.

Before you arrive, ask yourself:
💭 What emotion am I carrying right now?

If you’re feeling overwhelmed, numb, or just “off,” try:
• Writing a few thoughts down
• Letting yourself cry
• Talking with someone safe
• Naming your feelings without judgment
• Simply being around people who get it

Then come walk with us. 💚

📍 Where: Country Park – GSO
👉 Meet at: Picnic tables in Jaycee Park
🕙 When: Sunday at 10 AM
❄️ Winter Walk Time: Still 10 AM
🚶 Route: Easy, scenic 2.5-mile trail

You don’t need to have the right words. You don’t need to share if you don’t want to. Just showing up counts. All are welcome, all emotions, all paces, pets included.

Hosted in partnership with IMPAQT GSO and Carolina Aging Alliance Triad, creating space for wellness, connection, and care, one step at a time. 💚

#Respect #Inclusivity #CAA #IMPAQTGSO

events.description:
🌈 20 Years of Pride. One Historic Moment. 🌈

In 2026, Greensboro Pride celebrates 20 years of visibility, love, and community—and for the first time ever, we’re taking Pride to the streets with our inaugural Pride Parade 🎉🏳️‍🌈

This milestone year is bigger than a festival. It’s a statement.

We’re inviting sponsors and community partners to stand with us as we make history—supporting a free, inclusive Pride celebration that welcomes tens of thousands from across the Triad.

✨ Why Partner With Greensboro Pride?
• Unmatched visibility across digital, on-site, and stage media
• Prominent presence in our historic first Pride Parade
• Meaningful engagement with a diverse, loyal community
• VIP experiences and high-traffic vendor opportunities
• Alignment with 20 years of advocacy, impact, and celebration

Your support helps ensure Pride remains accessible, affirming, and powerful—not just for today, but for the future.

📣 Sponsorship opportunities are now open.
Let’s celebrate the past, honor the present, and march boldly into the future—together.

📩 Interested in partnering with us? Message us or visit our website to learn more.

events.title: 🎨 Paid Parking Reception
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us for the reception of Paid Parking 🌫️✨

The exhibition remains on view through Fqebruary 14 — stop by before it ends. 

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS

Paid Parking
Exhibition by Kate Robinson & Heidi Zenisek

On View February 10-14, 2026
Reception: Friday, Feb 13th | 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday, 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Run, don’t walk, to Iceland’s hottest new attraction. If you’ve never been to Gjaldskylda, you’re missing out! At least that’s what the hundreds of tourists tagging it on social media would like you to think. What is it, you ask? A parking lot. More specifically, it’s a parking lot that requires payment. Iceland has a specific online identity, carefully crafted by the Icelandic Tourism Board. The Land of Fire and Ice, renewable energy, Northern lights, geothermal spas… but as a trip to Gjaldskylda will illustrate, social media can be deceiving.

What is Iceland beyond the oversaturated Instagram photos of waterfalls and TikTok drone footage of mountains? This exhibition is a collection of works that inadvertently explore this question. Heidi and Kate have had transformative experiences in Iceland over the years, returning time after time to recharge their creative batteries and weave themselves into the culture. Upon return home, moments of these trips find their way into their practices. This show is the result of their curiosity about the overlaps in their work inspired by Iceland and how their similar experiences have manifested in different ways.

events.description:
CALL for COMMUNITY ART ➡️ NOW ACCEPTING SUBMISSIONS 📣

GPS’s Annual Community Small Work Show is BACK for its 3rd year 💪🏼 So join us in celebrating the creativity of our communities and SUBMIT YOUR WORK TODAY! 

Deadline to Submit: Feb 20, 2026 (11:59p)

Call not restricted to only local and regional GSO/Triad/NC artists! So please spread the word 🤗 (*current UNCG students are not eligible to apply) 

“GPS’s Annual Community Small Works” show explores the range of creative practices that flourish in Greensboro and beyond, bringing our communities together in the gallery. This exhibition activates our space with playful contrasting dimensions and diverse visual approaches across all artistic mediums. 

The only requirement is that all 2D/hanging 3D works are no larger than 18 x 24 inches - Everyone is welcome to apply. Artists are allowed to submit up to 2 works of art for the show. 

We hope you’ll consider submitting work, and also invite you to share this call with other artists in your circle - we’re gearing up to have a robust group of entries to showcase our local community and beyond💫

Mark Your Calendars! 🗓️
Exhibition: March 17-21, 2026 @ Greensboro Project Space 
Reception to Celebrate: March 20th, 6-8P

To submit, click the “Annual Community Small Works Submission Form” link in our profile 

*Note that all work should be dropped off/mailed installation ready/ready to hang

events.title: 🎨 American Pony Opening Reception
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us for the reception of American Pony by Constantine Kerasilis.🐎✨

Sadly we are closed today due to weather ❄️, so your chance to see this exhibition has passed, BUT we will be back to usual programming next week with yet another show!

************************
Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday, 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

American Pony is a group of work consisting of paintings, drawings, prints, and sculpture focused on the psychology of Americans under the rise of fascism. Simplified and abstracted within the metaphor of tortured ponies, this series is autobiographical in nature. Each work weighs morals, dreams, fears, self perception, and unity amongst Americans over the course of 2025. Specifically the year that changed countless lives affected by American politics and social climate. The world as I know it rapidly changes, for better or worse, these ponies are saturated in analogies to process change, they represent me and reflections of myself I see in so many others. Viewers are invited to think of themselves as I bear my mind for all to see and explore the complexities of their own mentality.

Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: 🎨 advance_base & moontypo Concert
events.description:
advance_base & moontypo are coming back to Greensboro with local support from likewindowpanes on Wednesday, February 25th 👾🎶🎹

Doors at 7pm and music at 8pm // $12 suggested 

This is going to be a really sweet show. We’re super thrilled to have these folks back in Greensboro. If you missed Advance Base for his winter tour in 2024 or Moontype last summer, this is your chance to see them both with Greensboro’s own, Panes. 

Advance Base is the melancholic soft rock recording project of Chicago, IL singer/songwriter Owen Ashworth (formerly of Casiotone for the Painfully Alone). Using a two-handed arsenal of electric piano, Omnichord, samplers, effect pedals & drum machines, Ashworth builds minimalist, heavy-hearted, nostalgia-obsessed ballads around his conversational baritone. The warm, electronic sound of Advance Base has been described as “lo-fi,” “depressed” & “weirdly uplifting.” Advance Base released Horrible Occurrences in 2024.

Moontype is songwriter Margaret McCarthy’s dreamworld come to life. Based in Chicago, Illinois, the band features Emerson Hunton on drums, and Joe Suihkonen and Andrew Clinkman on guitar. Quiet emotions that McCarthy explores with her bass become, with the band, as large and as musically complex as a mood itself: cathartic, angsty, and unmet. Moontype released their debut album, Bodies of Water, in 2021 to critical acclaim.

panes is minimal introspective music with an engaging, dreamy listlessness. ever careening toward some kind of undiscovered self epiphany.

Flyer by: likewindowpanes

events.description:
𝗝𝗼𝗶𝗻 𝘂𝘀 𝗼𝗻 𝗧𝗵𝘂𝗿𝘀𝗱𝗮𝘆, 𝗙𝗲𝗯𝗿𝘂𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝟭𝟵𝘁𝗵, 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗮 𝗻𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 𝗼𝗳 𝗰𝗼𝘀𝗺𝗶𝗰 𝘀𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱 𝗲𝘅𝗽𝗹𝗼𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 🔊🪐🎵🐴

𝙈𝙖𝙥𝙨 features Brian Haran (Ama Divers, Filthybirds; engineer on numerous records) handling electronics/oscillators/tapeloops, Nick Vanbuskirk on sax (Panes, Kill the Buddha, bedroom division, and countless others), along with Tyler Monroe behind the drums (plays with too many people to count). This project is 100% improv—noisy ambient colliding with avant jazz. brian.s.haran likewindowpanes _tyler.monroe_ 

𝙇𝙪𝙠𝙚 𝙎𝙘𝙝𝙣𝙚𝙞𝙙𝙚𝙧 is a Nashville based pedal steel guitarist who has worked with a variety of artists including Margo Price, Orville Peck, S.G. Goodman, William Tyler, and Futurebirds. His debut solo album of ambient New Age music created entirely on pedal steel, ‘Altar of Harmony,’ was released by Third Man Records in 2020. His new EP ‘For Dancing In Quiet Light’ is out now via Leaving Records. lukefromnashville 

𝙎𝙖𝙖𝙥𝙖𝙩𝙤 is the music project of upstate New York–based sound artist Brendan Principato, who weaves manipulated field recordings with lush electronic soundscapes exploring ecology and music. He has held residencies with the National and Alaska State Park Services, including 2023 near Juneau and 2022 on Fire Island. His recordings have been named Best Ambient on Bandcamp, and his live performances include collaborations with Laraaji, Don Slepian, Patricia Wolf, and Green-House. saapato

events.title: 🎨 Death Circle
events.description:
𝔇𝔢𝔞𝔱𝔥 ℭ𝔦𝔯𝔠𝔩𝔢, 𝔱𝔬𝔡𝔞𝔶 𝔣𝔯𝔬𝔪 5 𝔱𝔬 7 𝔓𝔐 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs 🖤

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. We’ll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there — 
Joshua & Bri

events.description:
Join us on Sunday, October 5th for a sweet lil show! 🐌🐌🐌

Doors at at 7/music at 8/$10 suggested 

We’re welcoming jacobcmckelvie on tour from Massachusetts, alongside Greensboro’s own kill_thebuddha & televoltmusic 

jacobcmckelvie has traveled the DIY touring circuit in the US and UK for over a decade, carving out a niche for himself as a songwriter and performer. Known for his off-kilter and whimsical “stream of self-consciousness” lyricism and charming stage demeanor, he keeps audiences engaged both as a solo performer or with his newly minted backing band, “the New Old Band”. 

He released his latest LP “A New Kind of Hat” in 2024 and has been touring in support of that since.

Jake’s music has been featured on Americana UK, WFMU, Daytrotter, No Depression and has opened for acts like AJJ, Jeffrey Lewis, Clem Snide, Jeff Rosenstock and Steve Forbert.

“If you like a literate singer-songwriter with a witty turn of phrase then Jake is your man.” - Americana UK

“It just slyly worms its way into your brain.” - No Depression

See ya at the show! 🧃

events.title: 🎨 Crisis Actress and Official Claire Music Performance
events.description:
⛓️FRIDAY, OCTOBER 3rd⛓️DOORS at 8/SHOW at 9pm/$12 suggested/See ya at the show! 🤠

Chicago-based crisis.actress_ crafts experimental pop that exists in perpetual evolution. At the heart of their sound are swirling keyboards, layered vocal harmonies, loops, and motorik guitar lines that propel their performances into unexplored territories. Guests are frequent, though the core ensemble includes Brooks Hannon on guitar and electronics, Max Hudson on synthesizers and vocals, Sam Pardo on bass and vocals, and Jake Keenum on drums. Their full-length debut, Return of the Puppet King, captures the band’s shared musical obsessions and commitment to experimentation. Recorded at the hatlowed Electrical Audio studio last fall, each track serves as a testament to their collective journey a dream realized through collaboration with countless talented friends who helped shape their vision.

officialclairemusic is one of Chicago’s finest new songwriters. She works with calm confession, deep wit, and the details of a poet– all on top of big, swaying hooks. The Chicago Reader praised her “incisive, witty lyrics… a combination of sensitivity and straight talk.” She’s also a calm shredder of guitar– both gritty and delicate. Claire grew up in the Chicagoland area. Her music caught the attention of former Wilco member, LeRoy Bach. Bach is a member of her live band, (as well as Brooks Hannon & Jake Keenum from Crisis Actress.) Official Claire’s most recent studio offering, Midnight Dickbiter,features five remarkable new compositions.

patoiscounselors : Based in Charlotte, NC. Friends and fans of music making more music.
We’ve performed with Protomartyr, Gustaf, Ceremony, Automatic, Young Widows, BODEGA, Ekko Astral, Public Circuit, Truth Club, Quintron, The Messthetics, Omni, Sheer Mag, Ibex Clone, Algiers.

mostly_mesh : Bass heavy post-punk darkwave with campy references to 80’s horror, BDSM tinged lyrics, and the processing of trauma. Macabre love songs drenched in lust, sarcasm, and corn syrup blood. Mostly Mesh invites you to don your black lipstick and make out while a slasher plays in the background.

events.title: 🎨 DAGGERTEETH Opening Reception
events.description:
DAGGERTEETH TOMORROW TOMORROW TOMORROW.
.
ARE Y'ALL PUMPED CUZ WE CAN'T WAIT TO OPEN OUR DOORS TO YOU TOMORROW!
.
Teeth don't lie. They bite, they tear, they mark what they touch.

Traditional printmaking techniques meet zine culture's raw urgency. Punk aesthetics slash through the white cube aesthetic and screams to be heard.

Every piece here carries evidence of hands—ink rolled, paper pressed, screens burned, images carved. The tactility of making things physical in a digital age. These works don't apologize for their rough edges. They weaponize them.

DAGGERTEETH celebrates artists who create outside institutional approval, distribution systems built on photocopiers and kitchen tables, aesthetics born from necessity and defiance. The underground made visible.

Sharp work for sharp teeth.

**February 21, 2026 | 5PM-8PM**

Irving Park Art & Frame

2800 Lawndale Dr. Ste 107, Greensboro, NC

events.title: 🎨 LEAF LITTER - ‘Abalone Variation 1–6’ by KT Wilson
events.description:
We’re thrilled to spotlight a truly captivating series in our current exhibition, LEAF LITTER—‘Abalone Variation 1–6’ by the talented artist KT Wilson. These original works are a celebration of the subtle, often overlooked wonders of nature, and we invite you to experience them in person at Irving Park Art & Frame.
Each piece in this series features a sustainably sourced abalone shell (pāua) from the pristine waters of New Zealand. Revered for their iridescent beauty and cultural significance, New Zealand abalone shells are harvested under one of the world’s most robust sustainability systems, ensuring that their natural populations are protected for generations to come. These shells, considered ‘taonga’ (treasures) in Māori culture, are not only a feast for the eyes but also a testament to the harmony of art and ecological responsibility⁠.
KT Wilson’s approach transforms each shell into a meditative focal point by mounting them in custom shadowbox frames. Rather than traditional fastenings, Wilson uses carefully woven textiles and thread to cradle each shell in place. This technique both secures the shell and highlights its natural form, color, and texture, drawing attention to the unique qualities of each specimen. The interplay between the organic shell and the geometric textile work invites viewers to contemplate the contrast and connection between the natural world and human creativity.
The ‘Abalone Variation’ series is a perfect fit for LEAF LITTER, our exhibition dedicated to the nuanced, often unnoticed aspects of nature. This show brings together artists whose work delves into the delicate, the intricate, and the ephemeral—reminding us that beauty is often found in quiet details. KT Wilson’s pieces exemplify this ethos, offering viewers a chance to slow down and appreciate the subtle complexity of natural materials and thoughtful craft.
.
Visit us at Irving Park Art & Frame to see KT Wilson’s work and explore the full LEAF LITTER exhibition. Support local art, sustainable materials, and the ongoing conversation between human hands and the natural world.
.
.
.
#ArtExhibition #ContemporaryArt #NatureInspired #KTWilson #AbaloneArt #SustainableArt #PauaShel

events.title: 🎵 Paul McDonald Live Performance
events.description:
Tonight, Sunday the 1st!
Doors at 6:30pm
Show at 7:30pm

Born in Alabama and baptized in the dive bars of the southeast, Paul McDonald first made noise with the Grand Magnolias, a roots-rock outfit, before catching fire in the public eye during American Idol’s 2011 run. When the bright lights blurred and the cameras turned, the man behind the voice slipped into the shadows where he did what real artists do: He lived, he lost, and he wrote. Retreating to Nashville, that holy city of reinvention, Paul stitched himself back together with worn boots, hard songs, and a new band called The Mourning Doves.

Now, in 2025, the giant has stirred the release of “So Long to the Dark Side” - a gospel tinged reckoning wrapped in cosmic Americana and lit with songs that sound like they were scribbled on the edge of a breakdown and sung back from the brink. Raised on Petty, Parsons, and pain, Paul McDonald is not chasing trends, he’s conjuring somethingolder and truer. His live performances are equal parts revival and rock

Evan Blackerby has something to say. He’s been relentless in working to cultivate his own musical voice and sound - that can only be described as alt-bluesy Americana, folkish-rock-esque, singer-songwriter. 

Evan’s first concert was Paul McCartney and he leans on influences of Otis Redding, Ray LaMontagne, Avett Brothers , and Jack Johnson . He began crafting songs in 7th grade. That year, he found an old Yamaha guitar (that he named Junior) and began learning a few chords. He has, over the years, has become more comfortable in his unpolished honesty and ability to craft a song worth listening to.

events.title: 🎵 Murphy and Paper Wings Live Performance
events.description:
JUST ANNOUNCED!
Friday, April 24th, 2026
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Murphy is bringing a trio and Paper Wings will wow the Flat Iron crowd for the first time! This will be an intimate seated show. Get your tickets now while you can!

Murphy Campbell has deep roots. Western North Carolina is a place her family settled centuries ago. Born in the foothills and coming from a long lineage of mountain folk - she seeks to draw up the remnants of a forgotten modal sound.

Long-time friends and collaborators Emily Mann and Wila Frank, known together as Paper Wings, dream up warm, pastoral folk songs suited to wandering through a forest or field, quiet contemplation, and long winding journeys. Furnished with delicate banjo and spellbinding harmonies so close you often can’t tell their voices apart, Frank & Mann deliver dynamic performances emboldened by the strength of their sincere songwriting. The duo have an uncommon ability to tastefully reference nostalgic sounds of American folk music while maintaining their own compelling style of artful and unpretentious lyricism. The strength and solitude one finds in the wilderness is a theme throughout their writing, and they lovingly transport listeners to open landscapes in which to find comfort and ask the questions which we all have in common.

The Diali Cissokho Trio brings together three internationally acclaimed musicians whose work blends deep cultural roots with emotional resonance. United by their devotion to the transformative power of music, each member of the trio brings a unique perspective, celebrating and crossing cultural boundaries with their mastery of West African rhythms and instruments.

Together, the Diali Cissokho Trio creates a powerful collective force, merging their individual expertise to uplift and inspire. Through their performances and teachings, they offer a profound, cross-cultural experience, using the universal language of music to heal, connect, and inspire.

events.title: 🎵 Deaf Andrews and The Barons Live Performance
events.description:
TONIGHT, THURSDAY THE 19th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Greensboro, come show up and show out for these traveling bands!
Flat Iron favorites Deaf Andrews are headlining, with Flat Iron first-timers The Barons firing things up at 8 PM. Two killer original bands you do not want to miss—be there!

Conceived in the daydreams of adolescence, Deaf Andrews has blossomed from their roots, into a unique sonic flower of indie pop rock. Emerging from Charlotte, NC, the band is the creative collaboration of five friends—Nick Hayes (vocals), Mike Sherrard (guitar), Ross Schumann (bass), Andrew Kingsley (guitar), and Braedon Nelson (drums)—each bringing their own unique perspective and energy to make music that is both personal and universal. 

With a dedication to pushing creative boundaries and connecting emotionally, Deaf Andrews prioritizes capturing the raw, electrifying energy of their live performances. The result is a sound that is both organic and direct, channeling the spirit of classic rock and roll while embracing the experimental freedom of modern indie pop. With each release, Deaf Andrews continues to carve out their own space in the musical landscape, offering listeners a rich and dynamic experience that is as thought-provoking as it is electrifying.

The Barons is a US alternative band comprised of lead singer and guitarist Peyton Alley, drummer Mathes Glymph, lead guitarist Josiah Ragland, bassist Kirtland Gray and saxophonist Jack Peacock. Forming in 2016, the Virginia natives’ repertoire include various sounds ranging from heavy-hitting indie rock to a lighter fair of introspective love songs. The Barons have amassed a following among headlining colleges and venues on the East Coast as well as supporting some bigger alternative acts such as Judah & The Lion, The Aces, Barns Courtney, and Catfish and the Bottlemen. The band has released a stack of singles since its founding and will embark on their first full album release in 2025, “Le Château”.

events.description:
Tonight, Tuesday the 17th!
Doors at 6:30pm
Show at 7:30pm

Happy Birthday, Abigail!

Dawn Landesis a singer-songwriter based in Chapel Hill who’s penned original songs for TV, film, and musical theater (ROW on Audible). Landes has toured internationally and supported artists like Jose Gonzales, Mary Chapin Carpenter and many more. Her newest album The Liberated Woman’s Songbook was produced by Josh Kaufman (Bonny Light Horseman) and highlights women’s activism throughout history. 

www.dawnlandes.com

Jess Klein came to this world to pull firebrand power and childlike joy from our darkest shadows. Her acoustic-driven, hook-laden story songs revel in the polychrome of human experience - joy, rage, sensuality and resilience. The New York Times calls her “A songwriter with a voice of unblinking tenacity.” Jess has toured the globe, performing in pin-drop listening rooms, cozy theaters and on raucous festival stages.  She is currently based in Hillsborough, NC by way of Boston, New York City and Austin, TX. Jess has toured nationally and internationally supporting Arlo Guthrie, Josh Ritter, Damien Dempsey, John Fullbright and Jonathan Byrd and has opened for such legends as Ani DiFranco, Steve Earle, and Alejandro Escovedo.  She has appeared on Good Morning America and NPR’s All Things Considered and has performed at the Newport, Winnipeg, Falcon Ridge and Philadelphia Folk Festivals as well as Fuji Rock Festival in Japan. 

Abigail Dowd 

Our fearless host who still enjoys music most when it’s embedded in real life, sitting around a fire or every third Tuesday night at the Flat Iron. Each month Abigail invites musician friends to fall into space with her and the audience for what she describes as an evening in her adopted living room. They collaborate, share new songs, play old favorites, make things up, make you cry, make you laugh and most of all make you glad you came

events.title: 🎵 Support Local Businesses for Backpack Beginnings
events.description:
Hey everybody!

Our friends at freemansgrubandpub reached out to us about joining in with chandlersdtgreensboro and some other great local businesses to donate 15% of our Sunday revenue to backpackbeginnings! We gladly accepted and will be joining the effort. 

These are trying times, but Greensboro truly is a special place for community action and we’re so happy to join the train. So come out to support us and any of the other local businesses supporting this effort. 

We’re at the gsocurbmarket every Saturday, which is always collecting donations to support SNAP, and our friends at cornermarketgso are also actively supporting folks giving away SNAP credits.

events.title: 🤝 Manga Mania Book Club - Screening of Cardcaptor Sakura: The Movie
events.description:
Join us for Manga Mania Book Club, a casual manga club for adults!

March's meeting will feature a screening of "Cardcaptor Sakura: The Movie" (2000) based on the classic manga by CLAMP.

"Sakura Kinomoto, a Card Captor, wins a game of chance and is awarded a trip to Hong Kong, along with her best friend Tomoyo and her rival, Syaoran Li. It turns out that the ancient rival of Clow Reed, the creator of the mysterious and powerful Clow Cards, summoned them, and she’s out for revenge. A battle ensues, and secrets are revealed about Clow Reed’s shady past and Sakura’s connection to him."

The film will be presented in Japanese with English subtitles. The film is rated PG.

📍 Hemphill Branch
📆 Wednesday, March 4
⏰ 6:30 pm

events.title: 🤝 Local Author Presentation: AlTonya Washington
events.description:
📖⭐ Local Author Presentation! ⭐📖
AlTonya Washington returns with a presentation on her new novel, "Zepha’s Place".

AlTonya is the author of more than 100 romance novels. She currently works in the library at Winston-Salem State University. Her new novel is a departure from romance as she enters the historical fiction genre. It is the story of Zepha Dodge, an entrepreneur at a time when Black women could not claim such an accomplishment. 

📚Books will be available for purchase. There will be a book signing after the presentation. 

ℹ Call 336-412-6199 for more information.

events.description:
Our celebratory program and presentation for National Storytelling Week has been rescheduled!

events.title: 🤝 An Evening at the Cotton Club
events.description:
✨ Step back in time to the golden age of jazz, poetry, and style during An Evening at the Cotton Club--a celebration of Black music, poetry, and culture inspired by Harlem's legendary nightclub! ✨

This program will feature singing, live music, spoken word, and poetry readings, all set against an elegant 1930s Art Deco atmosphere. Community members will have the opportunity to be entertained by guest performers and also perform during an open mic segment, take photos in front of a backdrop, and enjoy light refreshments as they experience the rhythm and pride of the Harlem Renaissance. 🎶🎷🎵

events.title: 🤝 Teach-In about the Police
events.description:
This past Saturday (March 11th), we commemorated the National Day of Action Against Police Terror by hosting a Teach-In about the Police, their Copaganda, their Tactics, and the Laws that empower them. We are grateful to our mapinduzi_252 comrades who not only taught alongside us but planned it with us too. We are grateful to the community of people who came out to learn and support. We shall have more events like this in the near future and we look forward to doing even more for the community going forward.

“Next Chapter Bookstore and Gift Shop is a Black-owned, woman-owned space designed to showcase Creatives and their creations. events.title: 🤝 End-of-Year Raffle
events.description:
🎁 End-of-Year Raffle Time!
We wanted to thank you for showing up and reading with us.
✨ At our next meeting — Sunday, Dec 7 at 12pm at scuppernongbooks — we’ll be hosting a raffle with three prize bundles.
Each prize includes:

📚 a Blind Date With a Book
🌿 + bookish goodies from local small businesses we love (boroughcoffeegso chandlersdtgreensboro thetinygreenhouse scuppernongbooks )

Here’s how to enter:
✨ If you’ve attended 6 or more meetings this year, just give us your name at our Sunday meet up.

✨ Can’t make it this weekend? Send us a DM to let us know you’ve hit 6 meetings, and we’ll add you to the raffle.

If you’re just now joining us, we’d still like to see you on Sunday! 

✨ Thanks for reading with us.

events.title: 🛍️ Next Event
events.description:
Merry Christmas & Happy Holidays 🎄 As we approach the end of the year , we are thankful for all of the love and support we’ve gotten thus far. It’s never easy starting something new, you’ve gotta just put your best foot forward and go. But with God, the right people around you and a community-centric town like Greensboro, there’s no way to lose ❤️ so I appreciate everyone who has shared a post, told a friend, or dragged a family member to one of our events. We’ve done a lot in 9 months thanks to y’all, looking forward to what the next year brings! 

And since it’s the giving season we figured we’d give you the date of our next event 😄 February 1st! More details and vendor information coming soon . Stay Tuned, Stay Warm, Stay Safe! 🫡

📸 | shottbyverde

events.title: 🤝 Repeal of Article 12, § 95-98 Campaign
events.description:
A couple years ago, two GRS comrades began working on a set of documentaries for @ UE150 NC Public Service Workers Union and its struggle against North Carolina’s Jim Crow-era legislation that bans public-sector workers and their unions from the right to collectively bargain. Such racist, sexist, anti-worker legislation contributes to NC having the worst conditions for workers in the *country*

But the workers and their supporters are fighting back!! We are in a political moment now where more and more people are understanding class dynamics and its role in oppression and exploitation. GRS joins UE150 and many others across the state in the struggle for a repeal of Article 12, § 95-98 of the NC General Statutes. Perhaps now more than any time in decades, a united, powerful movement to repeal the statute could actually win. Here are clips from the first short info/hype video!

SOLIDARITY WITH THE PUBLIC-SECTOR WORKERS’ STRUGGLE, AT HOME AND EVERYWHERE!!

#abolishNCGS9598
.
